title: Cells, Walls, Passages, Boundaries, and Invariants · Transfer Build
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers cells, walls, passages, boundaries, and invariants into a changed reciprocal wall and passage model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of reciprocal wall and passage model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy.
Carve a passage as one atomic reciprocal update between adjacent cells.
Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Topology Validation
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-topology-validation-1nt412" that uses case 3605 to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-topology-validation-1nt412
# Competency: maze-topology-validation.
# Case 3605: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_topology_validation_1nt412(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1nt412 = "1nt412-214tn1-3605"
    assert evidence_variant_1nt412
    evidence_axes_1nt412 = (
        "grid1nt412",
        "topology3605",
        "search214tn1",
        "event1nt4123605",
        "access36051nt412",
        "recovery214tn13605",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1nt412)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"
